figures.caption: Fig. 3. Single-cell CRISPR activation screens in human B cells identifies hundreds of gene targets 232 regulated by autoimmune risk loci. 233 (A) Schematic of SCANDAL sgRNA library design for autoimmune risk loci and control regions, single-cell capture and 234 identification of cis-regulatory targets in response to CRISPR-SAM perturbation. (B) Multiplicity-of-infection (MOI) of 235 SCANDAL library in GC B cells. (C) Relative frequency of tested loci with significant cis-regulatory effects on gene 236 expression within 500 kb. (D) Differential expression of genes within 500 kb for all targeted SCANDAL loci. Significant 237 locus-gene effects are denoted in red and blue. (E) Genome snapshot of CRISPR-SAM targeting at rs7441808, 238 including cis-regulatory effect on target gene RPBJ shown as curved line. (F) Expression levels of genes in (E) for cells 239 containing rs7441808-targeting or non-targeting control (NTC) sgRNAs. Points reflect single cell expression values, 240 diamonds indicate mean expression values, size corresponds to cell percentage the gene was detected, and Benjamini-241 Hochberg-adjusted SCEPTRE p-values are shown. (G) Number of SCANDAL locus-gene pairs with GTEX eQTL 242 support in different tissues. (H) Number of SCANDAL target genes mapped per trait for top 14 autoimmune traits, 243 classified as to whether they were targets for loci linked with multiple traits. Gene-trait pairs for eight target genes with 244 the most traits, including the number of independent SCANDAL cis-regulatory effects underlying the associations. (I) 245 Comparison between gene expression (GEX) and protein levels (antibody derived tags; ADT) cis-regulatory effects for 246 SCANDAL loci. Spearman’s rank correlation (ρ) and p-value are shown. (J) Changes in SCANDAL targets by genomic 247 distance to risk locus. (K) Absolute perturbation effect sizes for cis-regulatory targets from different studies. (L) Gene 248 ontology for 362 SCANDAL target genes. (M) Expression of SCANDAL targets across immune cell types. (N-O) 249 SCANDAL effects of either lowly expressed (N) or not detected at baseline (O) cis-regulatory target genes. (P) Change 250 in SCANDAL target gene expression between disease and controls in peripheral blood and disease-relevant tissues. 251 p-values from Student’s t-test are shown. 252

Fig. 5. Transcriptional activity and co-regulation of non-coding autoimmune risk loci. 387 (A) Chromatin accessibility and histone modification levels (CPM; counts per million) at distal SCANDAL loci (±250 bp 388 from SNP) with or without a mapped cis-regulatory target gene. CTRL denotes gene desert and distal control loci. p-389 values denote Student’s t-test. (B) Quantification of transcriptional activity at CREs (tCRE) using total 5' GEX RNA 390 counts or 5' TSS RNA counts. (C-D) 5' TSS RNA counts at distal SCANDAL loci before (C) and after (D) CRISPR-SAM 391 targeting, separated by whether a target gene was identified. p-values denote Student’s t-test. (E) Relative frequencies 392 of distal SCANDAL loci with and without significant change in tCRE activity (total GEX RNA) after CRISPR-SAM. (F) 393 CRISPR-SAM-dependent tCRE activity and target gene expression. Spearman’s rank correlation (ρ) and p-value are 394 shown. (G) Genome snapshots of CRISPR-SAM perturbation for rs10152590 and TSS of target TLE3, including tCRE 395 activity for cells containing non-targeting control sgRNAs and cells containing sgRNAs targeting rs10152590. (H) 396 Differential testing for SCANDAL CRE-CRE perturbation effects following CRISPR-SAM activation of distal CRE 397 elements. (I) Differential tCRE activity (total GEX RNA) after CRISPR-SAM perturbation of distal intergenic SCANDAL 398 risk loci. Significant effects determined by SCEPTRE shown in red. (J) Genome snapshots of CRISPR-SAM 399 perturbation for tCRE activity for cells containing non-targeting control sgRNAs and cells containing sgRNAs targeting 400 rs11501246. (K) Genome snapshot of CRISPR-SAM perturbation and Micro-Capture-C (MCC) at the CXCR5 locus, 401 including total GEX RNA counts for cells containing non-targeting control sgRNAs and cells containing sgRNAs 402 targeting 6 SCANDAL loci. Normalised expression of CXCR5 for each unique CRISPR-SAM perturbation is reported. 403 (L) Scaled expression (z-scores) for tCREs at CXCR5 locus depicted in (K) for cells containing sgRNAs targeting CRE1-404 3, the CXCR5 promoter, and non-targeting sgRNAs. 405

Fig. 1. A pan-autoimmune disease resource of fine-mapped GWAS risk variants. 108 (A) Trait frequency for 5,655 autoimmune risk SNPs from PICS2 and OpenTargets Genetics. (B) Predominant 109 population of SNPs across different ancestry groups (AFR = African, AMR = admixed American, EAS = East Asian, 110 EUR = European, SAS = South-East Asian). (C) Percentage of SNPs located within HLA/MHC cluster. (D) Trait 111 frequency for 853 multi-trait SNPs. (E) Shared trait associations for multi-trait SNPs visualised as a force-directed 112 graph. (F) Chromatin accessibility at autoimmune SNPs in immune cells. (G) Prioritization of 818 autoimmune SNPs 113 based on chromatin accessibility in B cells. (H) GC B cells chromatin accessibility (ATAC) and histone marks at 818 114 autoimmune SNPs centered on the middle of open chromatin peak. (I) Chromatin accessibility of 818 SNPs from B cell 115 open chromatin in non-B cell types. (J) Trait enrichment after functional prioritisation with B cell open chromatin. Red 116 or blue denotes significant increase or decrease by Fisher exact test. 117

Fig. 4. Distal regulatory gene targets of risk loci identified by SCANDAL and micro-Capture-C. 301 (A) Genomic context of SCANDAL risk loci (378) with mapped cis-regulatory target genes. TTS = transcription 302 termination site. (B) Genome snapshot of CRISPR-SAM targeting and Micro-Capture-C (MCC) for rs10174949, 303 including SCANDAL cis-regulatory effect on target gene ID2 highlighted as curved line. (C) Expression levels of ID2 for 304 cells containing rs10174949-targeting or non-targeting control sgRNAs in SCANDAL. Circular points reflect single cell 305 expression values, while diamonds indicate mean expression values for all cells containing individual sgRNAs where 306 size corresponds to percentage of cells in which the gene was detected. p denotes Benjamini-Hochberg-adjusted 307 SCEPTRE p-value. (D) Distance from SCANDAL risk loci to either nearest TSS vs TSS of experimentally-mapped 308 target gene. Dashed lines show the median distance. p-value from Student’s t-test is shown. (E) Frequency that gene 309 target for a risk locus is the nearest gene or not. (F) Genome snapshot of CRISPR-SAM targeting and Micro-Capture-310 C (MCC) for rs67289879, including SCANDAL cis-regulatory effect on target gene CCND3 highlighted as curved line. 311 (G) Expression of MED20, BYSL, CCND3 and TAF8 in cells containing rs67289879-targeting or non-targeting control 312 sgRNAs in SCANDAL. (H) Frequency of SCANDAL target genes within the HLA super-locus (chr6:28510120-313 33480577), including the number of independent risk loci for each gene. (I) Genome snapshot of SCANDAL targeting 314 of 8 loci and Micro-Capture-C (MCC) for rs9263871 and rs12212594 near HLA-C and HLA-B. (J) Scaled expression 315 (z-scores) of HLA-C and HLA-B for cells containing sgRNAs targeting 8 different autoimmune risk loci or non-targeting 316 control sgRNAs (NTC) in SCANDAL. 317

Fig. 2. CRISPR-Synergistic Activation Mediator (CRISPR-SAM) in primary human B cells to modulate 167 regulatory activity of autoimmune risk loci. 168 (A) Schematic of CRISPR-SAM activator, comprising catalytically dead Cas9 (dCas9) fused with VP64, activators p65 169 and HSF1 fused to MS2 coat protein (MCP) that binds MS2 loops in the sgRNA structure. Viral delivery strategy (RD114 170 retrovirus, GaLV lentivirus) is detailed for each construct. (B) Timeline for CRISPR-SAM activation in human GC B 171 cells. (C) Transduction efficiencies for individual and triple delivery of CRISPR-SAM components. (D) Mean 172 fluorescence intensities (MFI) for CD5, CD86, or CD19 after transduction of GC B cells with promoter-targeting sgRNA 173 for each gene and dCas9-VP64 only or dCas9-VP64+MS2-p65-HSF1 (CRISPR-SAM). MFIs are normalised to samples 174 with non-targeting control (NTC) sgRNA and CRISPR-SAM (n=3, ±SD). (E) Genome snapshot of CRISPR-SAM 175 sgRNA-targeting putative cis-regulatory element (CRE) upstream of CD83. GC B cell ATAC-seq and H3K27ac ChIP-176 seq are shown. (F) Representative histogram of CD83 surface protein expression after CRISPR-SAM targeting of CD83 177 putative CRE in GC B cells. Dashed line shows NTC MFI. (G) CD83 MFI values relative to NTC (n=3, ±SD) after 178 CRISPR-SAM at CD83 CRE. p-values from Student’s t-test are shown (* < 0.05; ** < 0.01). 179

Fig. 6. Massively parallel reporter assays and in situ prime editing identify loss- and gain-of-function 460 autoimmune risk variant effects at SCANDAL loci. 461 (A) Lentiviral massively parallel reporter assay (lentiMPRA) to quantify variant-specific effects on transcriptional activity. 462 (B) LentiMPRA transcription efficiencies (z-score) for reference (REF) and alternate (ALT) alleles with significant 463 variant-specific effects (p<0.05; paired sample t-test; n = 6). (C) LentiMPRA transcriptional efficiency (RNA/DNA, norm) 464 for REF and ALT alleles at SCANDAL regions, with cis-regulatory target gene annotated below. p denotes paired sample 465 t-test (n = 6, ±SD). (D) LentiMPRA transcriptional efficiency (RNA/DNA, norm) for REF (C) or ALT (T) alleles at 466 rs1432296. (E) Genome snapshot of CRISPR-SAM targeting and Micro-Capture-C (MCC) at rs1432296, including 467 SCANDAL effect on target gene REL as curved line. (F) REL expression after CRISPR-SAM perturbation for cells 468 containing rs1432296-targeting or non-targeting control sgRNAs. Circular points reflect single cell expression values, 469 while diamonds indicate mean expression values for all cells containing individual sgRNAs where size corresponds to 470 percentage of cells in which the gene was detected. p denotes Benjamini-Hochberg-adjusted SCEPTRE p-value. (G) 471 CRISPR-Cas9 Prime-Editing (PE7) in GC B cells to test risk variant-specific effects in situ via quantile-based sorting of 472 target expression and amplicon sequencing. (H) rs1432296 ALT (T) allele frequency and REL transcript mean 473 fluorescence intensity (MFI) in prime-edited GC B cells (n=5). Spearman’s rank correlation (ρ) and p-value are shown. 474 (I) REL ChIP-seq signal at SCANDAL risk loci and their SCANDAL target promoters. CREs are centred on open 475 chromatin peak or TSS. (J) Genome snapshot of REL-bound rs74405933 SCANDAL risk locus, with motifbreakR 476 prediction for A allele effect on REL motif. SCANDAL effect on target gene REL as curved line. (K) CD83 expression 477 after CRISPR-SAM perturbation for cells containing rs74405933-targeting or non-targeting control sgRNAs, with 478 formatting as detailed in (F). (L) LentiMPRA transcriptional efficiency (RNA/DNA, norm) for REF (G) or ALT (A) alleles 479 at rs74405933. (M) rs1432296 ALT (A) allele frequency and CD83 surface expression (MFI) in prime-edited GC B cells 480 (n=5). Spearman’s rank correlation (ρ) and p-value are shown. (N) Number of SCANDAL loci bound by REL, denoting 481 enrichment of REL-bound loci within each trait as compared to all SCANDAL loci with gene targets for this trait. (O) 482 Binding of SCANDAL target transcription factors (TF) to other SCANDAL loci with targets, showing if the bound locus 483 has the same trait as the locus controlling the TF expression. (P) Proposed model of genetic pleiotropy underlying pan-484 disease autoimmune risk. While cis-regulatory effects on TF expression may be mediated through a GWAS risk variant 485 associated with Trait A, the downstream targets of that TF include many non-coding risk loci (or their regulatory target 486 genes) with genetic associations for different traits. 487
